purpose: Provides three independent analytical approaches to measuring AI equity concentration risk that converge on the same conclusion of extreme and underestimated concentration
components: PCA: PC1 explains 45-50% of return variance vs 30-35% historical
ENB via Meucci entropy: momentum ENB at ~25-35 below 2007 and 2020 precedents
Factor correlations: momentum-quality tripled from +0.15-0.25 to +0.50-0.60
Convergence of three independent metrics indicates systematic risk model underestimation
current_assessment: All three metrics at or near historical extremes simultaneously. Multi-factor portfolios holding momentum and quality running ~2x intended AI exposure. Market-neutral portfolios carry ~15-25% unhedged AI directional exposure.
limitations: ENB comparison across time periods faces regime-change uncertainty
PC1 elevation could partially reflect genuine fundamental correlation not just crowding
Factor correlations could persist if AI-driven fundamentals justify co-movement
Three years of post-ChatGPT data too short for definitive factor attribution
